title: gouldia cordata var. molokaiensis
intro: Gouldia cordata var. molokaiensis, also known as Molokai gouldia, is a rare and endemic plant species found in the Hawaiian Islands, specifically on the island of Molokai. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, which are attributed to its leaves, stems, and roots, and has been traditionally used by local healers to treat various health issues. The medicinal properties of Molokai gouldia include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, sore throat, and skin infections. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and stems are often made into teas or infusions, and its roots are used to make tinctures and salves, which are used to promote wound healing and soothe skin irritations.
